metadata.archetype_justification: In-memory registry with full CRUD operations (register, renew, cancel, status update) exposed via REST endpoints.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: The application has no authentication enforcement. ServerRequestAuthFilter only logs client identification headers via Netflix Servo counters — it does not validate, reject, or enforce any identity. The filter chain calls chain.doFilter() unconditionally regardless of header presence or value.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication mechanism exists. No OAuth2, API key validation, mTLS, or any form of credential verification. Any client can call all endpoints without proving identity.
findings.recommendation: Implement API Gateway or service mesh authentication in front of Eureka server endpoints. Add JWT or mTLS validation to the servlet filter chain.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Implement API Gateway authentication or servlet-level JWT/mTLS validation.
blocker_remediation.target_state: All API calls require a validated machine identity with principal attribution in audit logs.

findings.title: No Scoped Permissions (Least Privilege)
findings.description: No authorization model exists in the application. All endpoints are accessible to any caller without any permission checks. There are no IAM policies, RBAC definitions, or role-per-service configurations.
findings.gap: No scoped permission enforcement. An agent identity cannot be granted read-only access to specific resources without inheriting broader privileges.
findings.recommendation: After implementing machine identity (AUTH-Q1), add method-level authorization using IAM policies or API Gateway resource policies to distinguish read vs write access.

compensating_controls: Deploy behind API Gateway with resource-level policies restricting agent access to GET endpoints only.
Use network-level segmentation to limit which callers can reach write endpoints.

findings.title: No Action-Level Authorization
findings.description: No action-level authorization exists. The application does not distinguish between read, write, and delete operations from an authorization perspective.
findings.gap: Cannot enforce action-level restrictions — an agent granted any access could register, cancel, or modify instances.
findings.recommendation: Implement fine-grained RBAC or ABAC in the servlet filter chain or via API Gateway method-level authorization.

compensating_controls: API Gateway method-level authorization (restrict agent to GET only at gateway layer).
Network-level controls separating read and write traffic paths.

findings.title: No Agent Identity Suspension Mechanism
findings.description: No identity management system exists within the application. Since there is no authentication, there is no mechanism to suspend or revoke individual agent identities.
findings.gap: Cannot isolate a misbehaving agent without taking down the broader platform or applying network-level blocks.
findings.recommendation: Once machine identity is implemented, add API key revocation mechanism or integrate with Cognito/IAM for identity lifecycle management.

compensating_controls: IP-based blocking at load balancer or firewall level.
Header-based blocking rules in a WAF or reverse proxy.

findings.title: Rate Limiting Disabled by Default
findings.description: A rate limiting filter (RateLimitingFilter.java) exists with a token-bucket implementation but is commented out in the default web.xml deployment descriptor. When enabled, it only covers read endpoints, not write operations.
findings.gap: Rate limiting not active by default. Write endpoints have no rate limiting. Runaway agent loops could overwhelm the server.
findings.recommendation: Enable the existing rate limiting filter and extend coverage to write endpoints. Deploy behind API Gateway with per-client throttle configuration.

compensating_controls: Uncomment rate limiting filter mapping in web.xml for read endpoints.
Deploy behind API Gateway with throttling configuration covering all endpoints.

findings.title: No Data Residency Controls
findings.description: The Eureka registry stores service instance metadata including hostnames, IP addresses, AWS metadata. No data residency configuration or region-restriction controls exist. Peer replication replicates data across configured peer nodes with no region boundary enforcement.
findings.gap: No data residency controls. Service instance metadata replicated across peer nodes could cross region boundaries without governance.
findings.recommendation: Document data residency requirements for service registry metadata. Configure peer replication to respect region boundaries.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, peer replication crosses regions without governance
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q2 conditional resolves to RISK-SAFETY for read-only scope because registry metadata is replicated across configured peers without region boundary enforcement, creating residency risk if an agent transmits this data to an LLM in a different jurisdiction.
compensating_controls: Restrict agent access to read-only operations against a single-region Eureka cluster.
Ensure LLM endpoints are co-located in the same region as the Eureka cluster.

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, Swagger, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema, or Smithy model files exist. The REST API is defined implicitly through JAX-RS annotations only.
findings.gap: No machine-readable API specification. Agent tool generation requires manual tool authoring.
findings.recommendation: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specification from JAX-RS annotations using swagger-jaxrs2 or similar tooling.

compensating_controls: Generate OpenAPI spec from JAX-RS annotations.
Manually author an OpenAPI specification based on resource classes.

findings.title: No Structured Error Responses
findings.description: Error responses use HTTP status codes only without structured error bodies. Most errors are empty responses or plain-text strings. No consistent error response format.
findings.gap: Agents cannot distinguish retriable errors from terminal errors. No error code, category, or retryable indicator.
findings.recommendation: Implement a Jersey ExceptionMapper that produces structured JSON error responses with error code, message, and retryable flag.

compensating_controls: Implement error-mapping middleware wrapping HTTP status codes into structured JSON.
Document error patterns externally for agent tool definitions.

findings.title: No Schema Versioning or Contract Testing in CI
findings.description: URL-based versioning (/{version}/apps) exists but no formal schema documentation, no breaking change detection in CI, and no consumer-driven contract tests.
findings.gap: No automated contract validation. Agent tool schemas could break silently when API responses change.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec diffing or consumer-driven contract tests (Pact) to CI pipeline.

compensating_controls: Pin agent tool definitions to a specific API version (v2).
Add contract tests to CI pipeline.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing or Structured Logging
findings.description: No distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, Zipkin). Logging uses Log4j 1.x with unstructured text format. No JSON structured logging. No correlation IDs.
findings.gap: Cannot trace agent-initiated requests. Logs are unstructured and lack correlation IDs.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation and migrate from Log4j 1.x to structured logging framework with JSON output and correlation IDs.

compensating_controls: Add servlet filter generating and propagating request IDs.
Migrate to SLF4J + Logback with JSON layout.

findings.title: No Alerting Configuration
findings.description: Netflix Servo metrics are defined (EurekaMonitors.java) but no alerting thresholds, CloudWatch alarms, or anomaly detection configuration exists.
findings.gap: No alerting configured for API degradation.
findings.recommendation: Configure alerting on Servo metrics (error rates, rate-limited counts) using CloudWatch or equivalent.

compensating_controls: Configure external monitoring to scrape Servo metrics and alert on thresholds.
Add health check endpoint monitoring.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC exists in this repository. Application packaged as WAR with no defined infrastructure configuration for deployment target.
findings.gap: Integration surface not defined as code. No peer review of infrastructure changes. No drift detection.
findings.recommendation: Define Eureka server deployment infrastructure (compute, networking, API gateway, IAM) as IaC.

compensating_controls: Manage infrastructure in a separate IaC repository with PR-based review.
Use AWS Config rules for drift detection.

findings.title: No API Contract Testing in CI/CD
findings.description: CI/CD pipelines exist (GitHub Actions) with unit test execution. No API contract testing, OpenAPI validation, or breaking change detection configured.
findings.gap: API-breaking changes not caught in pipeline. Agent tool bindings could break silently.
findings.recommendation: Add API contract validation to CI pipeline.

compensating_controls: Add manual API review step before releases.
Pin agent tools to specific API version and monitor for 4xx spikes.

findings.title: No Deployment Rollback Capability
findings.description: CI/CD publishes artifacts to Maven Central. No deployment rollback mechanism, no blue/green, no canary deployment, no feature flags.
findings.gap: Cannot roll back deployment to previous known-good state if a change breaks agent-facing APIs.
findings.recommendation: Implement deployment automation with rollback capability (blue/green or canary with automatic rollback triggers).

compensating_controls: Maintain previous WAR artifact for manual rollback.
Implement deployment rollback in hosting infrastructure.
